title: oxalis corniculata var. villosa
intro: Oxalis corniculata var. villosa, also known as the hairy wood sorrel or creeping oxalis, is a perennial herb belonging to the Oxalidaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms and its potential to reduce inflammation. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, urinary tract infections, and skin problems, due to its diuretic and antibacterial properties. Oxalis corniculata var. villosa can be consumed as a tea or used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, to harness its medicinal benefits.
